scope: worker RCA and minimal runtime/health-check fix

dispatch_hygiene.note: UTV2-778.json was repaired before lane start by adding a missing file_scope_lock array. This is dispatch hygiene only and is not part of the worker RCA logic.

rca_classification.env_config_issue: not primary; worker env is valid when local.env is loaded from the main repo
rca_classification.queue_starvation: not primary; no pending rows for worker targets, but heartbeat should still prove liveness

root_cause: The supervised worker was down before intervention: supervisor DOWN, child DOWN, last heartbeat 2026-05-05T12:09:37.184106+00:00, pending outbox rows 5.
The worker process can start with the configured environment, which rules out a hard startup failure.
In indefinite autorun mode, runWorkerCycles used maxCycles=0 but only slept when cycle < maxCycles; this caused an unpaced loop instead of polling at UNIT_TALK_WORKER_POLL_MS.

runtime-health treated Worker Supervision as failed using only stale distribution.process rows, even when fresh worker.heartbeat rows proved the worker was alive and there were no eligible target rows.
worker-alert-check could not run because top-level await failed under the repo's CommonJS tsx execution path.

pipeline_health.worker_verdict: DOWN - no runs or heartbeats in health window

worker_alert_check.failure: Transform failed: top-level await is not supported with the CommonJS output format

pipeline_health.worker_verdict: HEALTHY - heartbeat fresh, no eligible distribution rows processed

skipped_or_out_of_scope: runBoardPickWriter was not investigated or changed because the RCA did not show it as the same root cause.
Provider freshness, scheduler freshness, and Discord delivery freshness remain visible in runtime-health but were not changed because they are not the worker DOWN root cause.
The 5 pending outbox rows are outside configured worker targets, so they were preserved and not silently moved or dropped.
